goal: Mengganti palet Aurora Terminal menjadi tema gelap netral profesional dan memperbarui design tokens global.
summary: Phase 1 completed: theme tokens in `tailwind.config.ts`, `app/globals.css`, and `DESIGN.md` migrated from Aurora cyan/magenta to a neutral professional dark palette. Signal green/red reserved for BUY/SELL. TypeScript and ESLint pass; npm scripts aligned to standard runners.
stepSummaries:
1. Update color/shadow tokens in `tailwind.config.ts` to neutral dark palette; keep `buy`/`sell` as signal green/red, add/rename accent/info/warn tokens; remove or rename `aurora` shadow. [verified]
  2. Update CSS variables, `.btn-primary`, `.btn-danger`, badges, glow, shimmer, focus/selection, and body gradient in `app/globals.css` to neutral palette. [verified]
  3. Update `DESIGN.md` color spec and directional-color rule to reflect neutral theme. [verified]
  4. Run typecheck and lint to ensure token changes compile. [verified]

reviewerRationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

gateVerdicts.rationale: Steps 1-3 used targeted grep (proxy) to confirm removal of old colors, appropriate for static config edits. Step 4 ran real `tsc --noEmit` and `eslint` (syntactic) and both passed.
gateVerdicts.evidence: tailwind.config.ts grep: only rename comment; globals.css grep: 0 matches; DESIGN.md grep: 0 matches; `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` exited 0.

gateVerdicts.rationale: The phase goal was to replace the Aurora palette with a neutral terminal dark theme; the three design-system files now use the new palette and pass typecheck/lint.
gateVerdicts.evidence: Updated color tokens in tailwind.config.ts, globals.css primitives, and DESIGN.md spec.

gateVerdicts.rationale: Nothing deferred in this phase. Non-signal `buy`/`sell` usages in components will be addressed in Phase 2 as planned.
gateVerdicts.evidence: Phase 2 step list explicitly covers decoupling non-signal color usage across components.
